author: exploittheexploit
content:

author: _shemnon
content: I see you've found dbj's blogs. While cryptodevs may not have cryptographers this group does, and are aware of the issues and don't need the flashy hype around it. Your telling elides significant details.

author: exploittheexploit
content:
I don't "found", I probably know it longer than anyone else here. Need "flashy hype" when there is a need to respond to those who making dangerously wrong claims. 

I'm currently filing a national patent, I will have 12 months to initiate PCT at WIPO and after some time to file patent in other nations.  If anyone in the U.S.A or other countries members of WIPO trying to steal my work and claim patent, I have a bad news for them. Their trademark will be thrown out to the trash because even if I filed in another country first, PCT will use the national application filling so I can block globally any attempt to patent my cryptographic software which I designed for ultra-high post-quantum security.

There is no NIST algorithm that comes even close to the security I have. At the lowest tier I have smaller signatures then NIST has at the highest Level 5 tier (128-bit).

author: exploittheexploit
content:
The largest tier I offer is 16384-bit QUANTUM! You have no idea what's going on. Unlikely someone need this big key, but you can use today comfortably 512-bit QUANTUM. 

NIST has a strong incentive to limit at 128-bit (so as they claim) while ML-KEM on all levels in reality only provides 93-bit. NIST is a scam! **One of their standardized post-quantum algorithm got rekt by a guy on a weekend with a single notebook!!!**

Clowns like to depend on NIST. <:crazycry:932693133546889216> 🤡
